rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Peek at the next module before adding a second exercise
Between-module reading appears in two places
Cross-module artefact contracts must be explicit at both ends
rules_evaluated.evidence: M6 Prework (line 8): 'Walk in with both Module 4 (un-packaged) and Module 5 (packaged) run artefacts.' M6 Start here (line 23): 'The un-packaged Module 4 send-off. The packaged Module 5 re-run.' Neither names the stable identifiers from M5's contract table (m5/<task-slug> branch, reference.md, plan.md, verifier path, RUN-NOTES.md, session transcript path). M5 producing side is fully explicit; M6 consuming side refers to artefacts generically.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: M6 Prework or Start here should name the stable identifiers for the M5 artefacts it reads (e.g. 'm5/<task-slug> worktree, reference.md, plan.md, verifier at the path Phase 3 named, RUN-NOTES.md, session transcript at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/<session-id>.jsonl').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Session boundaries and working directories are designed, not implicit
rules_evaluated.evidence: M5 close says 'Open a new Claude Code session in the worktree' (the m5/<task-slug> worktree at ../<repo>-m5). M6 Start here (line 22-27) and Prework do not name the working directory in which the student opens the M6 session. M5 artefact contracts and M6 artefact contracts both reference the M5 worktree (e.g. './CLAUDE.local.md in the M5 worktree'), implying M6 continues there, but the student is never explicitly told where to cd or which session to open.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: M6 Start here or a brief 'Open your session' callout should state explicitly that M6 continues in the same ../<repo>-m5 worktree where M5's packaged re-send ran — no new fork needed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts — every produced file is a contract row in the maintainer block
Cross-module structural-failure rescue — body-prose callouts at module open when prior send-off can fail
Audit-discipline — walk M(N-1) close before flagging M(N) architecture claims as spec drift
Bring-or-scramble plain-stakes-line shape at module homework boundaries
